item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images of the room, the bulb, and the humanoid are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0), as the room image could not be located under a certifiably cc-compliant source, the bulb image was found here, which is under a non-compliant license, and the humanoid image is an artistic piece by Razvan Boar and is copyrighted. Thus, these images have been removed.

item.title: A nice variation.
item.content:encoded: Pitch haven's heavy religious themes are well constructed but the overload of it can often make it difficult to sink into each article. I've got to give it to you, this article feathers in pitch haven without making it seem abrupt and stands well enough on its own. The fact that you made an entirely unique piece featuring one of the major elements of pitch haven, and made it good enough to stand on its own is particularly impressive. A definite +1, this is a refreshing change of pace and a very enjoyable read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, I'm a game dev (well learning to be), and I was wondering… Can I have permission with copyright and what-not to make a game of this? It would be very nice! Please, message me if you will let me! Thanks, 1stpersononmars. BTW this will be my first game!

item.title: Staff Post -- Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time. ~ Conwell.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show whatever
- Hide whatever

Considering that Site-45 is apart of the Pitch Haven series and this does have the pitch haven tag on it, it only makes since to include a link to the hub in order to give some more information on the site and canon as a whole for people who are interested in learning more about this. I propose the following crosslink from this:

Elevators on Site-45-C are to be modified to restrict transport to Floor 24.

To the following:

Elevators on Site-45-C are to be modified to restrict transport to Floor 24.

If you have any other suggestions for crosslinks, please, let us know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Be sure to check out the rest of Pitch Haven Hub If you haven't already! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of those scips where I just NEED to come to the comments to guess what the freaking hell is going on, and nobody wrote a clear synopsis so I was left almost as confused as I was. Having said that, it's fantastic, and those paper-mache zombies remind me of that necromorph in Dead Space that wants to puke in your mouth (Pure nightmare fuel.)

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit, my first read-through I was fairly confused, but those logs really made it for me. That second one really got me in the feels. Enjoy your +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, reading through all of Pitch Haven in chronological order was a great idea. I liked all these skips when they came out initially, but seeing how they relate to one another, it's like reading them again for the first time. And this one seems the least related to the others, until I hit

The coat and ears gave it away.

and just jeezus. This is just a straight continuation of 1903.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right in my feels

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…so this Crowley is the same Crowley from SCP-2999?
…holy shit these two skips just got even more horrifying

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a bad thought, but the problem with that is that I'm male, and she's not… Plus she's basically a god, way older than I, and committed suicide from drowning in a river.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speculation (probably spoilers)
Probably wrong but...

F█████ is "Fantem", isn't it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, everyone else seems to have liked it enough, so I'm not sure my criticism will mean much, but to me it is much too long. The logs are good and are what really carry it; everything before then confused me horribly (so many sub-designations!) and the various different parts seem very disconnected. I'm left with no idea what's going on, but not in a good 'this is intriguing' way, but more a 'hang on, I don't see at all how this works' kind of way.

That's just my reaction, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you liked it! I might reference them in another skip, but I think I gave you all enough information to find out who F█████ is… At least partially.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Running a week behind, but this is a great SCP to come back to. Pretty much everythign about this is right, from the environment, to the horror, to the logs. I want to know more about A & F though. They seem pretty interesting,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… Well, Hel is a pretty cool interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: F█████ is a name. Dr. ██████ just stopped himself from saying "fuck" in his log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does the F-blackboxes stand for? If it starts with the "fuc" that the doc mentioned, I don't know the word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the sheer amount of storytelling and work put into this article, which is well seen. I am actually surprised this is not rated higher, as it can compete with many top rated "storytelling" articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ..Fantem, burn in hell. Or rather, Hel. Apparently the underworld needed some renovating. The boy with the stiched mouth is obviously Loki, Hel herself is the woman in the dress, and I'm going to take something of a stab in the dark as to the fisherman and wife being Aegir and Ra'n.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very impressive, I really enjoyed it. The description was slightly confusing for me and I wasn't very clear on how -Cs play into this but otherwise this is amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Level 2 guy: Hey sir, um, can I be promoted?
Level 3 guy: What? Why?
Level 2 guy: I have to know what happens next!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The bulb was a sort of sigil for SCP-1619. It was sapped, and broke when it got too dim, which created this. As for the rest… They aren't so much connected to each other, as they are connected to the artist. I don't want to say it, and I won't admit it if I can help it, but I know people can find out.

If you mean how they relate to each other physically, it's an artscape. They are all meant to follow the basic roles of flora and/or fauna, and interact as a real ecosystem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, sure. I'm fine. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An interesting and engaging story; I quite liked it. The tone needs a fair few tweaks, though. May I? (You can always revert if I overstep.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great job on the article! Reading it was a little confusing, but it all paid off with the log. It's a wonderful and original concept, and it immersed me from the beginning.

+ Spoiler
- ...Sort of

The only thing I would have asked for is a little more explanation on the relationship between the Artist and the lone sculpture, or, more specifically, the origins of the sculptures. Were they a horrible mistake created by the Artist to replicate his lost love? Are they a subconscious creation of his addled and destroyed mind? There's definitely some potential, but nothing worth risking the story over. In fact, it's kind of nice that you left it to the imagination.


+1 for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has been getting a lot of praise, so this is kind of hard to admit, but I have no idea what's going on with this article. I like the logs, but the description is just confusing to me. I feel like I'm missing the connection between the anomalous item and the resulting phenomenon and the connections between the different details (walking sculptures, living lamps, a plant that looks like paper, ink that kills you). Can someone explain it in simple terms?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well detailed, and even though quite long, was a great read; had me hooked from the first paragraph of the description. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most of this was just meh for me, it was a really long read to get to the good part. That being said, I did thoroughly enjoy the second log, it had the horror elements i enjoyed. The thing is, you couldn't really have the weight in the logs without the long wordy description and containment. Still, while I like parts of the log, there just isn't enough mass in the sheer volume to make it a "dense" read for me. Vote withheld for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Oh, and thanks for the help with describing Crowely's impromptu treatment. I had no idea what to do there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well done. Long but worth every word. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait a goddamn minute. Crowely is a chick?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, can't thank you enough. I'll get to them right now… By the way, I apologize if I came across as passive-aggressive in that post. Didn't mean to if I did. :P

E: Mainly fixed. Regarding the bulb, I meant to imply any light bulb would work, anomalous or not. Within SCP-1619 though, SCP-1619-2-As'd be hunting other -2s for their light bulbs so they can feed themselves by smashing them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
anything specific
hail the nitpicks

Floor B24 has become a habitat for several types of sentient autonomous sculptures, grouped into two categories, SCP-1619-1 and SCP-1619-2. Floor B24's environment contains various art supplies and furniture, which primarily consists of modern glass tables and office chairs.

You establish that B24 is a dimensional anomaly, than mention -1 and -2, than go back to describing the layout of B24, then go back to the critters. Switching the second paragraph with the third would help. Also the details about furniture look out of place right next to -1.

Floor B24 consists of various hallways, rooms, and observatories, but lacks any doors.

That made me think that there's just drawings of doors on the walls similar to what happened to exits from the floor, and I was puzzled about how everyone goes from room to room until the field log. Maybe mention something about empty doorways.

has the same appearance and texture of canvas paper.

Either "has the appearance and texture of canvas paper", or "has the same appearance and text as canvas paper". Former is better, imo.

French documents and sketches have been recovered from within rooms containing one or more desks.

This is kinda weird. Maybe "Sketches and documents written in French have been recovered from the desks present within SCP-1619". On than note, you use Floor B24 a lot, to the point that it becomes repetitive; maybe interchange it with SCP-1619 here and there.

are analogous female humanoid sculptures

Analogous looks weird here and I'm not sure what you mean by that.

Instances do not appear to notice or give

"Entities" or "sculptures" would fit better here. I think "instance(s)" as a standalone noun looks weird pretty much everywhere, it's always "instance(s) of SCP-1619-1" or "SCP-1619-1 instances"

Either the act of killing a SCP-1619-1 instance, or breaking a light bulb is sufficient in preventing an instance's bulb from dimming.

It's not very clear how that works. I get that when a -2 kills a -1, the bulb of -2 is re-lit, but what's with the breaking of a bulb? Do you break the bulb that is the head of -2 (which yeah would prevent it from dimming, seeing how it's broken, but that's a weird thing to mention), or do you break a non-anomalous bulb near the -2 and then -2 isn't dimming anymore because you scared it by breaking the bulb, or does the -2 needs to break a light bulb? Hm, actually I think it's the later, seeing how some -2s hunt other -2s, but it isn't immediately clear from the context.

and restrain victims to the ground.

This is kinda weirdly phrased

the attacking SCP-1619-1 will release 30 ml of paint into the mouth of the victim.

The 30ml part seems unnecessarily specific here. Is it 30ml per second, or total, and how was it measured, seeing how the paint also self-replicates? Imo just "will release paint into the mouth" would be fine.

Attempts of removing this caricature

"Attempts to remove this caricature".

SCP-1619-1 is mainly considered prey by most SCP-1619-2 instances. SCP-1619-1's ineffective hunting methods, slow speeds, and lack of interest in SCP-1619-2

"are mainly considered prey", "slow speed".

SCP-1619-2 instances that only attack SCP-1619-1s instinctively are designated SCP-1619-2-B and have a small hole in the middle of their face-plate, which releases a thin beam of light.

This would work better as two sentences: "[…] are designated SCP-1619-2-B. The constructs have a small hole in the middle […]". Also saying that -B "only attack SCP-1619-1s instinctively" implies that there are other -2 specimens that only attack -1, but not based on instincts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Real classic stuff, just wow. This has a lot going on but it all fits. I love that not only are there two narratives, but we learn the story behind A-blackbox and F-blackbox without learning what's actually going on with the skip. I mean, it actually resolves nothing, and it's beautiful that way. The description could be revised a bit for readability, I think (for instance, "a pH of 8.7" would sound a little better), but otherwise this is really, really great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, Crowely and Dr. Hayward have been consistent characters in my recent SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for bringing those up, I'll give it a look over when I get to a computer with a better typing apparatus. Is there anything specific, or is it just the description in general?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's fine, I was just kidding in chat, and they got fixed anyway. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Before I read this, is this is in any way connected to any of your other SCPs? Because if it is I'm going to spend the next X amount of hours getting as high as I can to work out their connections and meanings. Especially if they're related to the Bunny one, goddamn it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was pretty meh through the description and addenda, but the field log makes it. +1

There are some wording and tone issues in the description. They're all pretty minor, but there is a lot of them and it worsens the impression.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remember reviewing that in chat. Pretty fantastic.

I love how the amount of info is enough just to identify who everyone in this article is, but not in-your-face about it.

I also have a fetish for skips that kinda work from biological standpoint (anomalous and all), and the fact that there are two anomalies - perpetuum mobile lamps and predatory caricature paint, that just happen to interact with each other in the worst possible way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely spooked me, it took an additional read to imagine what happens to those who get "painted" in both senses of the word. +1 fersho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Usually, long skips don't pay off for me. I find them boring or cliche. This is neither. It delivers. It's interesting throughout, has a lot of payoff, and invites the reader to speculate more without feeling like it's holding something back. I like it a lot. It also thoroughly creeped me out as I read it in the dark with my cats. I also loved the tone of the recordings, they felt exactly like how people would really talk in-universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
TAKE EVERY LAST FUCKING UPVOTE I HAVE!
This, My friends, is what horror is supposed to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So you know, I actually vote on very few articles. If I don't legit feel repulsed or very impressed, I usually leave it neutral.

Here's looking at you, kid.

Fuckin'… CPR papermache zombies…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember reading this as a draft, and being shocked by how good the logs were (not that the rest of your work isn't fantastic, but I mean, these are something special).
The feeling's held.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it then, love it now. I also just realized that this is a prime example of why the Foundation doesn't use anomalous items or SCPs for personal gain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! Despite that I apparently leave some crits until right after you posted it. Ahem. (Sorry.)

Anyway, +1. I like the little ecosystem you have going on here.

item.title: Author Post.
item.content:encoded:
Just wanted to thank everyone who looked at this, and give credit to the artists. The photo of the empty room and the bulb are stock photos, and the picture of the SCP-1619–1 was taken from the humanoid category from the visual records.

However, regarding the two pictures featuring –2-C instances (this one and this), those are the MiCha Lamps (photo: V.Warin), and I’d like to thank the artists for allowing me to write about them. More information about these beauties can be found at http://www.houseofmicha.com/en/. I urge you all to check em’ out, they’re pretty awesome!

And thanks for reading! (≡O≡)
